evals.name: legal-contract-chunking-strategy
evals.prompt: I have a corpus of 10,000 legal contracts (PDFs, avg 20 pages each) that I need to make searchable for a legal research assistant. How should I chunk these documents for retrieval?
evals.expected_output: A concrete chunking strategy recommendation covering: (1) which chunking approach to use for legal documents (structure-aware/recursive preferred), (2) recommended chunk size and overlap, (3) how to handle the context-loss problem (contextual retrieval), (4) why fixed-size chunking is insufficient for legal text, and (5) consideration of document structure (sections, clauses, definitions).
expectations: Recommends structure-aware or recursive chunking over fixed-size for legal documents
Provides specific chunk size guidance (token range) with overlap percentage
Addresses the context-loss problem and recommends contextual retrieval or similar enrichment
Explains why isolated chunks from legal contracts are ambiguous without document context
Mentions the retrieval stack framing (chunk layer is first design decision)

evals.name: irrelevant-retrieval-results-diagnosis
evals.prompt: My RAG system returns results but they're often irrelevant — the chunks look topically related but don't actually answer the question. What's wrong with my retrieval and how do I fix it?
evals.expected_output: A diagnostic walkthrough using the retrieval stack: (1) identify the likely layer failure (retrieval strategy — dense-only missing keywords, or reranking absent), (2) recommend hybrid retrieval (dense + sparse + rerank), (3) explain why semantic similarity alone is insufficient for precision, (4) introduce the neural reranker as the precision fix, and (5) mention retrieval quality metrics (recall@k, MRR, nDCG) for measurement.
expectations: Uses the retrieval stack as a diagnostic framework to identify the failure layer
Identifies dense-only retrieval as a likely cause of topically-related-but-irrelevant results
Recommends hybrid retrieval (dense + sparse) as the fix
Recommends adding a neural reranker (cross-encoder) for precision improvement
Mentions at least one retrieval quality metric (recall@k, MRR, or nDCG) for measuring improvement

evals.name: multi-doc-spanning-structured-indexing
evals.prompt: I'm building a Q&A system over our company's internal documentation (wiki, Confluence, Slack archives). Simple questions work fine, but complex questions that span multiple docs fail. Should I add structured indexing, and if so, which kind?
evals.expected_output: A structured indexing recommendation covering: (1) confirm that flat retrieval is insufficient for multi-doc spanning queries, (2) compare the three structured indexing options (RAPTOR, GraphRAG, filesystem paradigm), (3) recommend the filesystem paradigm (L0/L1/L2) as the best fit for wiki/Confluence/Slack, (4) explain why GraphRAG is overkill for this use case, and (5) note the critical cross-reference requirement.
expectations: Confirms that flat retrieval fails on multi-hop/cross-document queries and structured indexing is warranted
Explains at least two of the three structured indexing options (RAPTOR, GraphRAG, filesystem paradigm)
Recommends the filesystem paradigm (L0/L1/L2) as the best fit for wiki/Confluence/Slack-style corpora
Notes the critical cross-reference requirement for the filesystem paradigm
Explains why GraphRAG is not the best fit (entity-relationship focus vs document navigation)
